programme.sub-title: S01 : E10 Inside Indonesia's Ultras [English Subtitles]

programme.sub-title: S01 : E19 Chinese Taboo: The Mental Health Conundrum
programme.desc: Mental health and illness are taboos in China. But with the psychological toll of COVID lockdowns, and intense pressure at work and in schools, China now wants to raise mental health awareness.

programme.title: Zoom in
programme.desc: A TV news magazine show packed with top stories from around the world. The weekly program zooms in on the details of trending news items and zooms out to give viewers the bigger picture. With in-depth analysis and current perspective, Zoom in takes you on a journey beyond the daily headlines.

programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation.

programme.title: Big Story

programme.title: Travelogue
programme.desc: Travelogue is a 30-minute travel program. Through the journeys of our intrepid hosts, we experience the beauty, culture and mystery of China, discovering how people live in this vast country and showcasing China's transformation to the world.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already "living in the future"!

programme.title: Inheritors
programme.desc: Inheritors is a multi-media product from CGTN that shines a light on China’s rich Intangible Cultural Heritage. Each 30-minute TV edition is hosted by a long-term expatriate in China who has a unique understanding of the country’s culture. He or she will introduce viewers to people who are dedicating their lives to its protection, inheritance, innovation, development and promotion.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 What Went Wrong: THE CONCORDE AIR CRASH

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E73 Catching Floridas Apex Predator
programme.desc: Top trappers from Florida Fish and Wildlife go to battle each day against dangerous nuisance alligators that lurk beneath the lush lakes and canals of Palm Beach.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E73 Mother Of The Everglades
programme.desc: Strap-in for a breathtaking tour of the history and wildlife of Lake Okeechobee, one of the largest freshwater lakes in the United States, and the source of life for South Florida's magnificent Everglades.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Should we save parasites? These species have a bad reputation but are an essential part of healthy ecosystems throughout the world.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Growing up in Montana, Casey Anderson came to love and appreciate wild animals at a very young age. He found a particular kinship with bears. One day he was faced with a fateful decision, leave and orphaned grizzly cub to die in the wild or save it. Now he is faced with that same decision again.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Follow a family of sperm whales in the heart of the Indian Ocean. Alongside a team of scientists, who have witnessed their most intimate moments over the past 7 years, we will lift the veil on the largest toothed predator on earth.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E30 Chungungo: The World's Smallest Otter
programme.desc: Meet a family of Chungungos, the world's mysterious, smallest otter. Living along the untamed coast of the southern Pacific Ocean, these furry, intelligent, and adorable creatures make their home in the wild of Chile.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Take a journey with famed underwater photographer Peter Kragh, as he explores the coast of California, and reveals some of the most spectacular life found under the sea.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Join ecologist Karen Odendaal as she gives us a tour of the Manyoni Private Game Reserve in the heart of Zululand, South Africa.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Throw on your wetsuit, and join filmmakers Kyle McBurnie and Alex Warham as they show off their California wild backyard, deep in the Pacific Ocean.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Take a trip to the north of Mexico with our host, Eréndira Hernández, as she shows off one of the most extraordinary landscapes in the Americas.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: The hustle and bustle of New York City is legendary worldwide, but right in its backyard lies a serene natural haven that most know very little about.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Meet Rene Araneda, a wildlife filmmaker and Patagonia explorer, as he takes you on a tour of his favorite place in the world: Torres del Paine.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.desc: In this episode, travel with scientists from the Earth Observatory of Singapore across the region to understand what is causing cities to sink.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Extreme Weather. Powerful Tsunamis.
programme.desc: For scientists at the Earth Observatory of Singapore, it is a race against time to understand the effects climate change will have on coastal infrastructures.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Urban Oceans. Super Corals.
programme.desc: Dive with scientists from the Earth Observatory of Singapore as they investigate the impact climate change is having on the ocean.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 Sea Level Rise and Engineering Solutions
programme.desc: South-east Asia is a unique part of the world, featuring low-lying countries, pristine islands, and vast coastal habitats.

programme.desc: El Dr. David Jeremiah, pastor principal de Shadow Mountain Community Church en El Cajon, California, entrega la Palabra de Dios a un mundo en constante cambio.

programme.title: Voz News
programme.desc: Karina Yapor y destacados periodistas analizan con profundidad las noticias de hoy.

programme.title: Sid Roth - ¡Es Sobrenatural!
programme.desc: Sid Roth, un judío creyente en Jesús, ha pasado más de 35 años investigando la cualidad sobrenatural de Dios. Buscando guiarte al caminar en lo sobrenatural.

programme.title: Joni Table Talk
programme.desc: El pastor Casey y Wendy Treat comparten su poderoso testimonio y revelan por qué la verdadera sanidad requiere tanto fe como acción.

programme.title: Ministry Now
programme.desc: La familia Lamb lleva a los espectadores de todo el mundo a Ministry Now. Lleno de fe radical, declaraciones alentadoras y consejos positivos.

programme.title: Aqua viva con Sergio Hornung
programme.desc: Aqua Viva presenta al Pastor Sergio Hornung cada semana compartiendo un mensaje cargado de fe, vision y herramientas practicas.

programme.title: Jason Lozano
programme.desc: La misión es llevar el mensaje de libertad a las naciones. A través de ganar almas y hacer discípulos podemos ayudar a las naciones a hacer una vida de libertad

programme.title: Voz News
programme.desc: Karina Yapor y destacados periodistas analizan con profundidad las noticias de hoy.

programme.title: Dios de Milagros
programme.desc: Experimenta el poder sanador de Dios, con las palabras ungidas del pastor Hernán Castaño en "Dios de Milagros". ¡Desata el mover de Dios en tu familia!

programme.title: Reflections
programme.desc: Disfruta de impresionantes escenas de muchas de las creaciones milagrosas de Dios.

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Quanzhou Nanyin: A Living Fossil in the History of Chinese Music
programme.desc: Nanyin, also known as “Quanzhou Nanyin,” is an ancient Han Chinese musical tradition whose singing style preserves the time-honored ethnic vocal techniques predating the Tang Dynasty, earning it the title of “a living fossil in the history of Chinese music.”The program features Yegor, a Ukrainian music enthusiast, visiting Master Su Shuyong, a Nanyin inheritor, to hear her decades-long stories of teaching this art. They also tour the Quanzhou Nanyin Troupe, where, led by its artistic director Zhuang Lifen, they learn about the basic performance formats and instruments of Nanyin. Additionally, the episode follows a post-1990s Nanyin performer, Zheng Mingming, as she sips tea with guests...

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: The Elegance of Luoyang
programme.desc: Luoyang, as the eastern starting point of the Silk Road, is a city with a rich history and a tradition of cultural inclusivity. As such, it enjoys a prominent position in the heritage of the whole world.In this episode, we’ll visit White Horse Temple to learn about its fascinating international background. At Longmen Grottoes, which were designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the year 2000, we’ll appreciate how the unique ancient splendor is being revived by 3D technology. At the city’s annual Peony Festival, we’ll explore the significance of China’s national flower and national dress.Invited to a Luoyang Water Banquet, we’ll learn something about the city’s culinary traditions....

programme.title: China Bound: Chongqing
programme.desc: Join us in southwest China's Chongqing, one of the world's biggest cities. From futuristic skylines and cyberpunk streets, to fire-breathing opera, spicy hotpot and kungfu tea, we're diving deep into this megacity the size of Austria.

programme.title: China Bound: Chongqing

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan

Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut

Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.5
programme.desc: Guarded by mountains in southeast China, Jiangxi channels rivers into Poyang Lake---the Yangtze's vital link. Starting from Mount Lu's misty slopes, watch and marvel as northern plains transition into an undulating land of hills and basins...

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port.With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads.In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites.Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its...

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: My Little Cricket Fighter
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore a way of entertainment that has existed for thousands of years in China - "Cricket Fighting". Music, Chinese Chess, calligraphy and painting, keeping flowers, birds, fish and insects -- eight traditional hobbies of Chinese people. "keeping insects" is the last one, but it is unique to China. Although the insects are small, the related culture has been developed for thousands of years by Chinese people. It contains the unique cultural psychology, folk customs and aesthetic values of the Chinese people.

programme.title: Inner Mongolia’s Frozen Wonderland
programme.desc: In this episode, we feature the Winter Nadam Fair and winter sports in Hulunbuir, Inner Mongolia. By late December, the Hulunbuir Grassland is blanketed in ice and snow. Even in the bitter cold of around 30 degrees below zero, the grassland is alive with a festive bustle.

programme.title: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing
programme.desc: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing delves into the historical origins and cultural background of Fengdu Ghost City, and also features the Zhongyuan Festival celebrations and ancestor-worshipping rituals held here. These activities reflect the local people’s positive and optimistic outlook on life and death, as well as the great importance they attach to their ancestors, families and family traditions.

programme.title: China's Ancient “Wall Street”: Pingyao
programme.desc: In this episode, we head to Pingyao – the ancient "Wall Street" of China and a historic banking hub. Enclosed by ancient city walls and seemingly frozen in time, this entire city, together with two nearby ancient temples, offers a one-of-a-kind adventure at this UNESCO World Heritage Site!

programme.title: Aerial China:Beijing
programme.desc: China’s capital of Beijing is a bucket list city for any self-styled world traveler, but let’s be honest, not all of us have the time! Not to worry, because we’re about to embark on an aerial journey over the nation’s heart, giving you a crash course on this massive hub of culture, international exchange, and technological innovation. If you’re afraid of heights, this may be the one time it’s okay to look down!

programme.title: Jingmai Wanderings: A Symphony of Humans and Tea
programme.desc: As a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mount Jingmai embodies the ecological wisdom of forest–tea symbiosis and the rich cultural diversity of its ethnic communities. It plays a vital role in biodiversity conservation and the preservation of traditional tea culture.This episode follows the stories of representative individuals to explore three key themes — heritage preservation, ecological sustainability, and youth empowerment — revealing the unique charm and sustainable development model of Mount Jingmai.

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: Seafaring Tales: Fu Ships
programme.desc: In past centuries, transporting goods along the Maritime Silk Road could be a hazardous undertaking. Ships often faced fierce storms which blew up on the high seas. To reduce the danger, China’s shipbuilders came up with a solution. They developed Fu ships, commonly known as junks, incorporating a special watertight bulkhead technology. Their revolutionary design allowed the ships to withstand the highest winds and roughest waters.The Maritime Silk Road was an ancient trading route that connected China to Southeast and South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa. As a key passageway for trade and cultural exchange, it thrived for well over a thousand years, bringing Chinese tea, silk,...

programme.title: Yangliuqing New Year Painting Art
programme.desc: On May 20, 2006, Yangliuqing New Year Paintings were named as part of China’s national intangible cultural heritage. The New Year painting tradition can be traced back many centuries, to the Ming Dynasty and beyond, reaching the height of its popularity in the Qing Dynasty. There are no restrictions on the themes of the paintings, and there is no blessing they cannot convey. Yangliuqing New Year Paintings are loved all over the country for their beautiful details and vivid colors—but most of all, for the good wishes they impart to anyone who sees them.

programme.title: Suzhou Fans

programme.title: Seafaring Tales: Fu Ships
programme.desc: In past centuries, transporting goods along the Maritime Silk Road could be a hazardous undertaking. Ships often faced fierce storms which blew up on the high seas. To reduce the danger, China’s shipbuilders came up with a solution. They developed Fu ships, commonly known as junks, incorporating a special watertight bulkhead technology. Their revolutionary design allowed the ships to withstand the highest winds and roughest waters.The Maritime Silk Road was an ancient trading route that connected China to Southeast and South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa. As a key passageway for trade and cultural exchange, it thrived for well over a thousand years, bringing Chinese tea, silk,...

programme.title: Yangliuqing New Year Painting Art
programme.desc: On May 20, 2006, Yangliuqing New Year Paintings were named as part of China’s national intangible cultural heritage. The New Year painting tradition can be traced back many centuries, to the Ming Dynasty and beyond, reaching the height of its popularity in the Qing Dynasty. There are no restrictions on the themes of the paintings, and there is no blessing they cannot convey. Yangliuqing New Year Paintings are loved all over the country for their beautiful details and vivid colors—but most of all, for the good wishes they impart to anyone who sees them.

programme.title: My Little Cricket Fighter
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore a way of entertainment that has existed for thousands of years in China - "Cricket Fighting". Music, Chinese Chess, calligraphy and painting, keeping flowers, birds, fish and insects -- eight traditional hobbies of Chinese people. "keeping insects" is the last one, but it is unique to China. Although the insects are small, the related culture has been developed for thousands of years by Chinese people. It contains the unique cultural psychology, folk customs and aesthetic values of the Chinese people.

programme.title: Inner Mongolia’s Frozen Wonderland
programme.desc: In this episode, we feature the Winter Nadam Fair and winter sports in Hulunbuir, Inner Mongolia. By late December, the Hulunbuir Grassland is blanketed in ice and snow. Even in the bitter cold of around 30 degrees below zero, the grassland is alive with a festive bustle.

programme.title: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing
programme.desc: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing delves into the historical origins and cultural background of Fengdu Ghost City, and also features the Zhongyuan Festival celebrations and ancestor-worshipping rituals held here. These activities reflect the local people’s positive and optimistic outlook on life and death, as well as the great importance they attach to their ancestors, families and family traditions.

Their revolutionary design allowed the ships to withstand the highest winds and roughest waters.The Maritime Silk Road was an ancient trading route that connected China to Southeast and South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa. As a key passageway for trade and cultural exchange, it thrived for well over a thousand years, bringing Chinese tea, silk,...

programme.title: Yangliuqing New Year Painting Art
programme.desc: On May 20, 2006, Yangliuqing New Year Paintings were named as part of China’s national intangible cultural heritage. The New Year painting tradition can be traced back many centuries, to the Ming Dynasty and beyond, reaching the height of its popularity in the Qing Dynasty. There are no restrictions on the themes of the paintings, and there is no blessing they cannot convey. Yangliuqing New Year Paintings are loved all over the country for their beautiful details and vivid colors—but most of all, for the good wishes they impart to anyone who sees them.

programme.title: Miao Embroidery: History in Stitches
programme.desc: According to legend, during a migration by the Miao ethnic group, a female chieftain wanted to keep a record of all the mountains and lakes she passed along the way. The means she chose of doing so was by stitching the images on her dress. By the time they reached a suitable place to settle, her clothing was covered with embroidered designs. This, it’s said, was the origin of Miao embroidery. As the story tells us, the designs in Miao embroidery are closely related to the natural world and daily life. The Miao people have a special fondness for nature. So, as well as depicting their daily farming and work activities, they copy birds, flowers, mountains, and grass onto their clothing. Over...

programme.title: Miao Silverware: A Shining Epic
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the fascinating world of the silver-working art of the Miao ethnic group. Join us, and hear the stories and see the works of four remarkable individuals. From a national-level inheritor who is preserving the traditional craft, to an artist infusing personal flair into his designs; from a female silversmith imbuing her work with feminine warmth, to a young artisan inspiring a new generation, we investigate the rich heritage, cultural significance, and evolving potential of Miao silverware.

programme.desc: In this episode, it’s Miao New Year, and that means multitudes of merrymaking! Fishing, drinking, bullfighting… Join the host Min-Zhui Lee, among the magnificent mountains of Leishan, where it’s all happening for the ethnic Miao.

programme.title: Valentine’s for the Pumi People
programme.desc: In this episode, we record the grand celebration of the traditional Pumi Valentine's Day in Luoguqing, Lanping County, Yunnan Province. Every year, on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month, young men and women from the local community gather on the vast meadows of Luoguqing to take part in singing and dancing activities in pursuit of marriage. During the program, the host interacts with local people of the Pumi and other ethnic groups, showcasing the traditional plateau lifestyle of the Pumi people, their distinctive firepit culture, as well as the community’s brand-new look in the new era. All these scenes unfold against the lively backdrop of the Valentine’s Day activities, fostering a...

programme.title: Valentine's with the Snake King
programme.desc: Every year on Qixi Festival (Chinese Valentine's Day), the town of Zhanghu in Nanping, Fujian Province hosts a folk custom event: the Snake King Parade. Local residents carry the statue of the Snake King from the temple, along with specially prepared pythons, and parade through the streets and alleys. Wherever the procession passes, residents and shopkeepers greet them with firecrackers and burn incense to pray, hoping to receive the unique blessing of the Snake King. This distinctive folk activity is rarely seen elsewhere across China. Taking this event as its highlight, the episode leads audiences on a deep dive into the towns and villages of northern Fujian, delivering a highly...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China : Shanghai
programme.desc: Balancing delicately on an alluvial plain and the Yangtze estuary of China’s east coast, much of Shanghai-literally meaning “on the sea”-sits at an average sea-level elevation of about 4 meters. Bisecting the city is the Huangpu River, dividing it into Puxi (west bank) and Pudong (east bank), where you’ll find its famous international airport. Join us as on an aerial cruise as we journey upstream towards one of the world’s most recognizable skylines.

programme.title: Kesi
programme.desc: Silk is a classic symbol of Chinese culture. It helped opened up economic and cultural exchanges between East and West and witnessed the spread of Eastern civilization to the world. As a representative of traditional Chinese silk art, kesi is renowned for its highly ornamental weaving techniques of highlighting warps and breaking wefts, and has been deeply loved by people for thousands of years. In folk culture, it enjoys the reputation of “an inch of kesi is worth an inch of gold.”

programme.title: Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe
programme.desc: On the occasion of the 50th anniversary of diplomatic relations between China and the EU, Inheritors of China launches a special multimedia series titled “Dream Builders: Stories from China and Europe.” Taking culture as the starting point, the series responds to public discourse by telling the real-life stories of Europeans living in China. Through their perspectives, it vividly illustrates how tradition and modernity spark mutual inspiration between China and Europe—showcasing a relationship of shared growth, empowerment, and enduring partnership.The series travels through cities including Beijing, Shanghai, Jinan, and Datong, focusing on Europeans of different ages and...

programme.title: The Sichuan Zhuqin
programme.desc: Sichuan Zhuqin is a national intangible cultural heritage in China. Nowadays, its performances are constantly innovated, with new styles such as ensemble emerging. Creators have broken traditional performance methods, integrating Zhuqin with traditional Chinese instruments like the guzheng and erhu, and also experimenting with contemporary music forms such as rap and folk rock, aiming to make this traditional art appeal to more young audiences.

programme.title: Living Arts of Huizhou
programme.desc: This is Huangshan in eastern China. In this city, you’ll encounter one of China’s most traditional building styles: Hui-style architecture. How does Hui style set this region apart? What’s life like for people living in these houses? With these questions in mind, we’re embarking on a journey to decode Hui-style architecture.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - So the great rivers may live - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 So the great rivers may live - Part 2
programme.desc: Yann Arthus-Bertrand thus continues his world tour and his inventory of the planet with the great rivers that suffer from man's excesses as a common thread. " So the great rivers may live" is the discovery of places, men and women who are fighting so that our waterways simply have their place on Earth. Thanks to his genius, man is correcting his mistakes: the Aral Sea, which once disappeared, is coming back. In New York, millions of people have drinking water thanks to the natural virtues of the forest.

programme.desc: Massage studio will show you the best massage techniques in places which will make you dream. Let us take care of you! Lay down, relax and forget all your problems.

programme.desc: Turn down the lights, kick your shoes back, take a cup of tea... You're on MyZen TV, time to relax.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 6 - Self-massage for the leg
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Self-massage for the leg
programme.desc: No time to relax and get a massage? These exercises let you relax by massaging yourself.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Joint relaxation
programme.desc: The joints are the first parts of the body to react badly to stress, therefore it is essential to relax and relieve them as often as possible. Some ankle and knee rotations, combined with calm, controlled breathing will allow you to soothe your joints. With a few simple, well-targeted exercises, this session will bring you deep relaxation.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 1 - Exercises to activate the muscles
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Exercises to activate the muscles
programme.desc: Need to regenerate your body? Take the time to make some exercices to decompress and find a peaceful harmony.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 6 - Self-massage for the back and shoulder
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Self-massage for the back and shoulder
programme.desc: Bad posture and sagging shoulders are behaviors which can cause our body to suffer. MyZen TV offers a series of four exercises to massage your own back and shoulders. Practicing these simple and effective exercises can easily allow you to relax.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.desc: The call of the Forest is about discovering places but above all it is about meeting those who protect this priceless heritage. Forests are essential to mankind. They capture CO2, promote the formation of clouds and therefore rain, protect the soil from erosion and provide a habitat for wildlife. From Madagascar, to France, to Cameroon, or even Eritrea, Yann Arthus-Bertrand sets out to discover the most beautiful forests, those who live in them, those who protect them and those who live off them.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The cheese menu
programme.desc: Today, Julie sets on the roads of France to talk about an essential product on French tables: cheese. More than a gourmet craze, more than an end-of-meal treat, cheese is associated with French gastronomy, and is also recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO. With nearly 1,000 varieties (soft cheeses, cooked, blue-veined, pressed cheeses…), the French are the biggest consumers of cheese in the world!

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Martial Arts and meditation
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Martial Arts and meditation
programme.desc: Today, we will meet in Santa Monica in the hub of the last healthy trends. Val will make us discover Erewhon, the biggest organic and hipster supermarket in Los Angeles. She will then meet Troy, a guru of healthy consciousness, for an outdoor class that mixes martial arts and meditation. Then, on the legendary "Muscle Beach", the beach of all athletes, we will meet David, an action movie double, for a very aerial workout.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.desc: Val takes us to discover Palm Springs, the trendy and designer city in the heart of the California desert. In the heart of a private collection, we will discover all the flora of this region: an outdoor plant museum, one of the largest collections of cacti, and even tequila flowers. Then we'll head to the Paséo Hotel, one of the zen and design hotels of Palm Springs, where we'll meet the chef of the restaurant who will concoct his signature dishes. We will end our day with a relaxing massage with crystals.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.desc: We meet again in the market garden of Romainville, for a special Halloween episode. In one of our first episodes, we learned how to sow a pumpkin seed. The pumpkins, which I had installed in my vegetable garden, have grown well now that we are in autumn. And that's good because today we're going to show you how to dig a pumpkin to make a beautiful Halloween lantern.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare delicious potato cutlets accompanied by a creamy vegetable purée. The cutlets are crispy on the outside with Japanese breadcrumbs and tender on the inside, while the celeriac puree adds a touch of sweetness and lightness. This dish is perfect for a comforting and tasty vegetarian meal.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Creamy cauliflower soup
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare a creamy, comforting cauliflower and celeriac velouté. This mild, delicate soup is spiced up with condiments like paprika, garlic and oregano, and a touch of olive oil for an even more gourmet texture. An ideal dish for a light starter or a warming dinner.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Paul John Elliott
programme.desc: Without really knowing why, Paul Elliott will change his life from cartoonist to massage therapist. Aspired by success, money, success, he will create several clinics and will eventually lose the human aspects and lose himself. A new turn, a new meeting and Paul will finally resume his dreams of painting. Follow Paul in his tumults and his happiness found.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.desc: They are beautiful sisters, they are passionate about nature and they have a very strong common point: entrepreneurship. After years of evolution in consumer sectors, discover Annabelle and Valérie who knew how to listen to their passion to create a fitness and surf club (for Annabelle) and a zero waste grocery store (for Valérie).

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Véronick Desmarais
programme.desc: The everyday work is not always easy and it is sometimes difficult to get by. If there is one thing that Véronick has learned from her experience and those around her, it is because she is able to surpass that and to embark on her dreams and plans to become a massage therapist. You will believe in your abilities too.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Violaine Courtois
programme.desc: Everything started from the dream of her parents, she becomes a veterinarian. In her career that she launched backwards, Violaine will meet people who will take her to a different path of life. The chance will be at the rendezvous but it is especially the courage to resume studies and create his company that will build his project. Follow the encounters and travels of Violaine that brought her to fasciatherapy and nonviolent communication.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 2
programme.desc: The incredible diversity of flora and fauna, the fight against climate change, the balance between growth and sustainable development: these are all battles being fought on five continents. Yann Arthus-Bertrand takes us on this expedition. We're off to Gabon to discover the Ivindo National Park and Port-Gentil, then on to Sri Lanka in Kandi. Back to Gabon we go to Libreville, then Lambaréné to talk about turtles and the health of the local people. Finally, off to Dhaka-Bangladesh.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Lower body relaxation
programme.desc: This exercise aims to release all the tension you can feel in your legs and feet. Then, with a series of muscle contractions and decontractions, you will deeply relax your legs and feet. You'll notice you'll feel much better overall.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Get in contact with your needs
programme.desc: Need to regenerate your body? Take the time to make some exercices to decompress and find a peaceful harmony.

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 1
programme.desc: How can we reconcile the development needed to sustain the lives of nearly seven billion people with the protection of the Earth's natural resources? Yann Arthus-Bertrand and his team have chosen Gabon to continue their survey of the planet. We'll be discovering the Kongou Falls, the Langoué clearing with their elephant in Gabon. Then in Colombia, we will go on the island of Malpelo with their shark. Back to Gabon we will discover the Minkébé National Park and the bats in Franceville. We will also go to India in Hyderabad, which has convinced farmers to use no pesticides and no GMOs in their cultivation.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Citrus flavors
programme.desc: Julie takes you through the Pyrénées Orientales, then Corsica, before making a stop on the Côte d'Azur, and concludes her journey between Rungis and Paris... all to explore the world of citrus fruits! These fruits add flavor, zest, and seasoning to any recipe, making them indispensable in the kitchen. They bring the warmth and sunshine of the South of France and the exoticism of distant lands. While we're all familiar with lemons, clementines, and grapefruits, Julie introduces us to a thousand other varieties.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Topanga Canyon, AMBA Yoga
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Topanga Canyon, AMBA Yoga
programme.desc: Val goes to the heart of Topanga Canyon, California, a favorite spot for yoga addicts and nature lovers. Here she will experience AMBA Yoga, a kind of hollistic yoga for women. We will also attend a Sound Bowl session, a Tibetan practice considered the ultimate in meditation. In the heart of Venice Beach, we will discover the "Plant Lab", a new high-tech application that records the vibrations of plants to create music. Finally, Val will present us the largest concept store of rare crystals in South America.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Hot Springs
programme.desc: Still in Palm Springs, we will discover the Parker Hotel, the most trendy and design hotel in town. A French chef will take us to cook in the middle of nature. Then, Val will go to Desert Springs, a town known for its natural hot springs. At one of the many spas in town, Val will test one of the springs and even attend a Sound Bowl session.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Growing mushrooms at home
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Growing mushrooms at home
programme.desc: We meet at the Romainville market garden centre to learn how to grow mushrooms at home. Some varieties, such as button mushrooms, oyster mushrooms or shiitake mushrooms, grow quite easily in mushroom houses. But you too can do it. And today we will show you how to make a miniature version of this kit to grow oyster mushrooms at home.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Mashed potatoes with vegetable gravy
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Mashed potatoes with vegetable gravy
programme.desc: In this recipe, we prepare mashed potatoes in vegetable stock. The mashed potatoes are soft and melt-in-the-mouth, while the richly flavored broth of bell bell pepper, carrot, zucchini, celeriac and tomato enhances the whole.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Sweet potato bruschetta
programme.desc: In this recipe, we reinvent the classic bruschetta by replacing the bread with a roasted sweet potato base. Garnished with tomatoes, guacamole, chilli pepper and a drizzle of olive oil, this healthy and tasty bruschetta is perfect for an aperitif or a light starter.

programme.desc: The TV show Earth in Sports will immerse you into the point of view of different athlete who are practicing extreme or peaceful sports at the best spot all around the world. You will discover the most beautiful landscapes and some of them are nearly impossible to reach.

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Carine (Nice)
programme.desc: After twenty years as a lawyer, Carine decided to give in to her desire to help others heal. Her inner journey led her to discover a passion for naturopathy as she rediscovered her love for nature and learned the healing qualities of the plants around her. Combined with a deep understanding of astrology and nutrition, she is now able to help others live better and find their own path.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Virginie (Lyon)
programme.desc: Virginie’s beautiful handwritten lettering decorates the walls and windows of many shops in and around Lyon, France, as she lives out her passion of finding beauty in every stroke. A former waitress, Virginie was struggling to find her calling when she suffered a life-altering horse accident that almost left her handicapped. At only 30, she was forced to put her whole life into perspective. She finally found herself through a love for graphic design and lettering, combined with a desire for freedom that came with working independently. To motivate other women, she has also launched “Ladyboss”, a network of inspiring women entrepreneurs.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Laetitia (French Alps)
programme.desc: While pregnant with her daughter, Laetitia, a former journalist, experienced a strong desire to change lifestyles and move to the countryside. Remembering the teachings of her grandmother, she began to venture outdoors into the surrounding Alps and study the different plants at her fingertips. Today she holds a wealth of knowledge about the medicinal and aromatherapeutic value of plants that she applies to making natural products. With utmost respect for the environment, Laetitia gathers only what she needs in order to make herbal infusions.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Louise (Oleron Island)
programme.desc: At only 20 years old, Louise is full of confidence and already achieved a personal dream of hers to share her passion for nutritious and locally-sourced ingredients on the island of Oleron, France. Repurposing a camping car, she set up a brunch and tea-time spot where the guests can hand-pick their ingredients in the adjacent organic fields. Louise transforms the ingredients into favourite creations of hers and even joins her father for boat trips when she needs to stock up on fresh fish for the menu!

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 1
programme.desc: In this instalment, Yann Arthus-Bertrand lifts the veil on the products people buy. Almost everything we buy has a direct impact on our environment. We head to Japan to talk about the impact of new technologies, then to the Republic of Congo in Bukavu for the coltan harvest. How does our total consumption affect the environment? What permanent brands are we imposing on our planet? How can we differentiate between useful and useless purchases?

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 2
programme.desc: Above all, these reports are an opportunity for Yann Arthus-Bertrand to take stock of the planet and denounce certain human behaviors that are at the root of dramatic, even irreversible, environmental and climatic degradation. Off to Tokyo, then Kenya, to an animal sanctuary. Then on to Mount Fuji to learn how to live there, and all the plastic that clogs up the seas. Reports will focus on everyday products, chosen for their destructive production practices for both people and the environment.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The taste of bread
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The taste of bread
programme.desc: Today, Julie takes you on a bread trail to uncover the dedicated artisan bakers and millers. Her adventure will lead her through the Libourne region, then onwards to the North of France, the Ardèche, and the Aisne, where bakers have transformed bread into a powerful catalyst for social cohesion. While some individuals may avoid bread, particularly due to gluten-related concerns, others have chosen to reposition it at the forefront of the dining experience by championing its nutritional benefits. This commitment is shared by several chefs whom Julie will meet at the conclusion of her expedition.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Ojai Valley Lavender Festival
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Ojai Valley Lavender Festival
programme.desc: Val Kahl is in the heart of Ojai, the lavender town, where she takes a tour in Ojai Valley Lavender Festival and tastes drink, gluten-free cookies and organic cooking products made of lavender. She later found herself in the middle of lavender field while stopping by the Frog Creek Farm. After working out on the hot power yoga around palm trees in Caravan Outpost, she heads out to Gratitude Rock for an organic fresh juice tasting with an avocado ranch view.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Japanese pagodas and Coconut Massage
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Japanese pagodas and Coconut Massage
programme.desc: Val goes to Nicholas, a master of enlightenment, at his wellness ranch. Here, Val will try out some new high-tech relaxation sessions. She will then go to one of the zenest hotels in California, where she will participate in a yoga session in a Japanese pagoda, followed by a coconut massage. She will test a Cryo-Café, a new concept store that mixes coffee break and cryotherapy session. Finally, she will attend a Theremin session, a very special relaxation ceremony.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Thinning seedlings
programme.desc: We meet in the market garden of Romainville to continue gardening together. In our previous episodes, we sowed chives and radishes. Today, we're going to learn how to take good care of them. And as a bonus, we'll show you a great technique to easily get even more plants in your garden. Let's get started!

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Risotto with asparagus
programme.desc: In this recipe, we prepare a creamy asparagus risotto, a delicate and refined dish. Arborio rice is slowly simmered in a flavorful broth, then combined with crisp asparagus and melted Parmesan. A hint of lemon and fresh herbs enhances this delicious and comforting meal.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare delicious potato cutlets accompanied by a creamy vegetable purée. The cutlets are crispy on the outside with Japanese breadcrumbs and tender on the inside, while the celeriac puree adds a touch of sweetness and lightness. This dish is perfect for a comforting and tasty vegetarian meal.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Juliette (Morbihan)
programme.desc: Juliette has long been aware of the delicate beauty of nature. For years she spent her life on the sea, organising regattas for the traditional sailboats that are famous in the French region of Morbihan. Her appreciation for the elements made for a smooth transition as she decided to leave the water behind and return to the earth. Learning to shape a ball of clay into a graceful and elegant object, she discovered a passion for ceramics. Today, she lives from her creations and makes pieces that reflect their surroundings, the stunning Gulf of Morbihan.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Anna (Brittany)
programme.desc: Anna’s unusual background provided inspiration for her life change. Her career began as a wolf trainer for cinema and it was during these years that she developed her deep love for animals. After being tired of seeing them used as props and constantly in cages, she decided to leave the world of show business behind and start an eco-friendly sheep and goat pasture. To further share her passion for these gentle animals, Anna has opened a conservatory farm where visitors can experience first-hand the joys of being close to nature. She also makes amazing goat cheese!

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Cristina (Spain)
programme.desc: At 31 years old, the last thing Cristina expected when she went to see the doctor about a blocked ear was to leave the consultation with a diagnosis for Breast Cancer. This young and passionate sociologist decided to apply the same philosophy to her treatment as she did when surfing: when a wave crashes down on you, get up and try again. Through her healing journey she deepened her love for the ocean and also discovered the therapeutic capabilities of yoga for cancer patients. Today she shares her practice through surf and yoga retreats and is specialised in oncological yoga, helping others heal.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Idoia (Spain)
programme.desc: Idoia thrives on weaving together the sources of inspiration that surround her. Heavily influenced by the natural beauty of her home region of Galicia, Spain, she creates unique woven pieces from diverse and beautiful materials. Idoia’s pieces represent her vision of a modern woman, drawing from the traditions and experiences of those who perfected the craft before her and also pushing to innovate and adapt her creations for the modern world. Her recent collaboration with luxury fashion house Loewe is a dream come true for this modern artisan.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 4 - Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 1
programme.desc: With Bangladesh as a running thread, Yann Arthus-Bertrand meets several people who have changed their lives to dedicate themselves to a major project. In Bangladesh, Yves and Runa Marre have created the Friendship Foundation. Among other things, they’ve created a floating hospital installed on an old barge bought in France. Julie Andersen, an ex-advertising executive from New York has come to South Africa to defend sharks with her Shark Angels Foundation. In North India, Kartik Satyanarayan is helping to stop the dreadful exploitation of the lippus bears (dancing bears). He’s created Wildlife SOS, a preserve to rehabilitate the bears that have been rescued and to give work to the men of...

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - USA: The end of oil - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 USA: The end of oil - Part 1
programme.desc: "The end of oil" is the amazing story of a raw material which has revolutionized man's life in just a century. But today, oil reserves are depleting. In his crazy race for always more production, Man didn't manage to preserve this precious energy that took million years for the Earth to give out. To tell the adventure of oil, Yann Arthus-Bertrand and his team went to the United States, the country which depends the most on black gold. At the start of the 21st century, it is now urgent to realize that oil resources are limited. It's today that we have to act and get organized, in order to prepare the new era to come. While all our economies are based on the petrol barrel's price, we'll...

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - A la carte grills
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 A la carte grills
programme.desc: If you're a nature enthusiast and a fan of outdoor cooking, the upcoming season is perfect for barbecue enthusiasts! Our journey will take us to Aube, nestled in the heart of the Brévonnes forest, and then to the Massif des Bauges, where grilling and snow create a unique combination. Afterward, we'll explore the Libournais region, where we'll uncover cooking techniques that make use of vine shoots, which are plentiful in the area. Just a short distance from the vineyards, we'll arrive at another corner of Gironde, the Blaye region, where the cooking method remains consistent but features a distinctly local ingredient: eel.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Cryotherapy & Vinyasa Yoga
programme.desc: It's in an episode full of surprise that we meet with Val Khal in the heart of Los Angeles for a visit to the new trendy wellbeing boutique Springs. This new concept store offers the opportunity to make Vinyasa yoga wearing a trendy outfit, treat yourself to a revitalizing juice detox before heading to a Cryotherapy session without leaving the venue! For a relaxing time after an intensive Vinyasa yoga session, Val takes you to a session of Fire cupping where the heath is ideal for getting rid of muscular pains from day-to-day stress.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Acro Yoga on a Skyscraper
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Acro Yoga on a Skyscraper
programme.desc: Val takes us to Los Angeles, on the roof of one of the most prestigious hotels in Beverly Hills for a session of acro yoga, aerial acrobatic yoga. Then, in the heart of Downtown, Val will experience a glass slide down one of the tallest towers in California. She'll also try SandBox Fitness, the trendy indoor surfing class, before trying Lazerfit, a new way to work out that combines warm-up, stretching and laser tag.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Cutting a tomato plant
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Cutting a tomato plant

Come on, let's see how they have grown.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Daniel Hautot
programme.desc: He had a real professional rise, quickly moving from commercial to sales manager. But the awareness quickly catches up with him too: what is the purpose of all this? Is it a real pursuit of happiness? Daniel keeps his acquired skills and puts them to the benefit of his passion for yoga. Follow Daniel until the opening of his yoga & community space.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Guillaume Nivelle
programme.desc: To make a profession representative of his personality is the discovery made by Guillaume at twenty-six. After several years as a bartender and crossing a dark period, Guillaume discovers nature and falls in love with it. Accompany Guillaume in his passion, in the middle of a refitted nature.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laura Belfadla
programme.desc: Laura will not tell you the whole truth because she does not want a system where you are told what to do. After several years as head of business development in law firm, Laura changes her life and creates the plant academy. Let yourself go for a healthier life.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laurence Sala
programme.desc: It took a burnout and several miscarriages to Laurence to take a new path. It's finally a bad thing for a good. Throughout her ordeals, Laurence will know how to listen to her needs, her desires and her passions. With a surprise, follow Laurence in her life as a mother and naturopath.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 4 - Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 2
programme.desc: With Bangladesh as a running thread, Yann Arthus-Bertrand meets several people who have changed their lives to dedicate themselves to a major project. In Bangladesh, Yves and Runa Marre have created the Friendship Foundation. Among other things, they’ve created a floating hospital installed on an old barge bought in France. Julie Andersen, an ex-advertising executive from New York, has come to South Africa to defend sharks with her Shark Angels Foundation. In North India, Kartik Satyanarayan is helping to stop the dreadful exploitation of the lippus bears (dancing bears). He’s created Wildlife SOS, a preserve to rehabilitate the bears that have been rescued and to give work to the men of...

programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 A pleasant train ride …
programme.desc: We meet in order to settle down pleasantly and correctly in the train and to relax by breathing to spend a pleasant journey.

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Life'n'One
programme.desc: In this episode, Gabriel will start her day with some vegan cuisine. She will experience facial yoga at the Life'n'One holistic wellness center, before meeting Katrin from Active Escapes for a Wake surfing session in Dubai Marina.

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Al Qasr
programme.desc: Gabriela starts her day with a boat ride through the canals of the majestic Jumeirah Al Qasr Hotel. She then meets with chef Gabi for a healthy cooking class, as well as a fitness influencer with whom she will go on a fitness tour. She will end her day with a Thai massage in the hotel's beautiful, lush garden.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Seashells on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Seashells on the menu
programme.desc: We will start this journey near La Rochelle to learn a little more about the breeding of the famous bouchot mussels but also a very particular technique to cook them. We will then go on a tour of France's best oysters: towards Cancale, Arcachon, Montpellier and finally Oléron to learn about the different breeding methods and to find out more about all the species in France. Julie will cook a fricassee of berniques, the little Chinese hats that cover the rocks of the coast, as well as pink clams that are little known to the public. We will continue our journey in Finistère to meet Sylvain, the only abalone breeder in the country, and then to Méru where there are still workshops magnifying...
